response:
Here are comprehensive unit tests for the `is_valid_email` function using pytest. The tests cover happy paths, edge cases, and error cases:

```python
import pytest
from your_module import is_valid_email  # Replace 'your_module' with the actual module name

def test_happy_path_valid_emails():
    # Valid emails
    assert is_valid_email("user@example.com") == True
    assert is_valid_email("firstname.lastname@domain.co.uk") == True
    assert is_valid_email("user+tag@example.org") == True
    assert is_valid_email("user123@sub.domain.com") == True
    assert is_valid_email("user@123.123.123.123") == True  # IP address domain
    assert is_valid_email("user@[123.123.123.123]") == True  # IP address in brackets
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.name") == True  # TLD with multiple words

def test_edge_cases():
    # Edge cases
    assert is_valid_email("") == False  # Empty string
    assert is_valid_email(" ") == False  # Only space
    assert is_valid_email("@example.com") == False  # Missing local part
    assert is_valid_email("user@.com") == False  # Missing domain
    assert is_valid_email("user@example") == False  # Missing TLD
    assert is_valid_email("user@example..com") == False  # Double dot
    assert is_valid_email("user@-example.com") == False  # Leading hyphen
    assert is_valid_email("user@example.com-") == False  # Trailing hyphen
    assert is_valid_email("user@example.c") == False  # TLD too short
    assert is_valid_email("user@example.COM") == False  # Case-sensitive TLD
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain..com") == False  # Consecutive dots
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com.") == False  # Trailing dot

def test_invalid_characters():
    # Emails with invalid characters
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com!") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com@") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com#") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com$") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com%") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com&") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com*") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com(") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com)") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com[]") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com{") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com}") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com<") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com>") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com'") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com;") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com:") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com\"") == False  # Invalid character
    assert is_valid_email("user@domain.com<") == False  # Invalid character

def test_case_sensitivity():
    # Test case sensitivity for local part and domain
    assert is_valid_email("User@example.com") == True  # Mixed case
    assert is_valid_email("user@example.COM") == False  # TLD case mismatch
    assert is_valid_email("USER@EXAMPLE.COM") == False  # TLD case mismatch

def test_ip_address_domains():
    # Test IP address domains
    assert is_valid_email("user@[192.168.1.1]") == True  # Valid IP in brackets
    assert is_valid_email("user@192.168.1.1") == True  # Valid IP without brackets
    assert is_valid_email("user@[192.168.1.1].") ==
